faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Quick answers about Wordle and how our hints work.

faqItems.question: What is Wordle?
faqItems.answer: Wordle is a word guessing game where you have 6 attempts to guess a hidden word. After each guess, tiles change color: green means the letter is correct and in the right position, yellow means the letter is in the word but in the wrong position, and gray means the letter is not in the word at all.

faqItems.question: What is the best starting word for Wordle?
faqItems.answer: Popular high-performing starting words include CRANE, SLATE, TRACE, and ADIEU. These words contain the most frequently used letters in English (E, A, R, S, T, L, N) and help you eliminate or confirm key letters on your first guess.

faqItems.question: How many Wordle games can I play here?
faqItems.answer: Unlimited! Unlike the official daily Wordle, our site lets you play as many games as you want in any word length from 4 to 11 letters. No account or download required.

faqItems.question: What word lengths are available?
faqItems.answer: We offer Wordle games in 8 different word lengths: 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, and 11 letters. The classic Wordle format is 5 letters, but you can try shorter or longer words for a different challenge.

faqItems.question: Is this the official Wordle game?
faqItems.answer: No. WordleHint is an independent fan site offering unlimited Wordle-style games, hints, and strategy guides. The official Wordle is published daily by The New York Times. Wordle is a trademark of The New York Times Company.

faqItems.question: What are the best strategies for solving Wordle?
faqItems.answer: Start with a word rich in common letters (like CRANE or SLATE). Use your second guess to test new letters rather than confirming known ones. Pay attention to letter position — a yellow letter tells you the letter exists but needs to move. Avoid reusing gray letters. In later guesses, think about common word patterns and endings (-TION, -IGHT, -OUND).

faqItems.question: What is Wordle Hard Mode?
faqItems.answer: In Hard Mode, any revealed hints must be used in subsequent guesses. If a letter turns green, it must stay in that position. If a letter turns yellow, it must be included in your next guess. This prevents random guessing and forces you to build on what you know.

faqItems.question: How do I improve at Wordle?
faqItems.answer: Practice with our unlimited games across different word lengths. Learn common letter patterns and word endings. Study our strategy guides on letter frequency, vowel placement, and elimination techniques. Start with shorter words (4-5 letters) and work your way up to build pattern recognition skills.

faqItems.question: What is today's Wordle hint?
faqItems.answer: We publish 5 progressive Wordle hints every day for the current NYT Wordle puzzle. Visit our Today's Hint page to see clues about letter composition, vowels, starting letter, ending letter, and the word pattern — without spoiling the full answer.

faqItems.question: Where can I find daily Wordle hints without spoilers?
faqItems.answer: WordleHint provides a 5-level hint system that lets you reveal clues one at a time. Start with a vague hint about the word's letter makeup, and only reveal more if you're still stuck. The full answer is always hidden behind a spoiler button so you never see it accidentally.

faqItems.question: Does WordleHint show the Wordle answer for today?
faqItems.answer: Yes — but only if you choose to see it. Our hint page for today's Wordle includes the answer behind a clearly marked spoiler button. We recommend trying our 5 progressive hints first before revealing the full answer.
